name: Golden path: vulnerable dependency prioritized and tracked to IT ticket closure
description: Software-supply-chain narrative framed for CTOs and application owners: a critical CVE aligns with internet-exposed workloads; SecureWatch merges intel, SLA math, CAB-friendly ticket payload, and evidence of patch validation—stressing orderly change management without exploit detail.

simulated_timeline.synthetic_narrative: OSINT-aligned stub ties CVE DEMO-9000-LAB (synthetic advisory token) to customer-facing storefront container running on internet edge cluster DEMO-EAST-CLUSTER.
Platform inventories revenue-impact tags, uptime windows, rollback owners—language suitable for CAB cover sheet.
Integrated ITSM artifact opens with prioritized queue, SLA clock, deterministic patch bundle reference (fixture).
Post-deployment scanner stub closes finding; residual posture notes transient compensating WAF tightening for weekend coverage.

payload.title: DEMO: Critical dependency with edge exposure footprint (synthetic)

payload.summary: Patch demo dependency CVE-DEMO-9000-LAB on edge storefront workloads

events_emitted.headline: Intel synthesized into CIO-ready risk statement
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Articulates urgency as business continuity + brand trust—not vendor fear.

events_emitted.headline: Finding prioritized by exposure × severity matrix
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Metric transparent to auditors: math, not opinion.

events_emitted.headline: IT ticket shows governed execution
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Board sees integration into existing change tooling rather than rogue scripts.

expected_agents_triggered.triggered_reason_synthetic: CVE alignment and exposure correlation drive intel agent workloads.
Critical dependency issue couples with SOC validation and CAB orchestration stubs.

expected_remediation.summary: Execute emergency-compatible patch path, document rollback, validate externally observable surface, retire compensating WAF rules after stabilization.

expected_autonomous_remediation.synthetic_action: Auto-open P1 IT ticket with CAB packet, SLA timer, stakeholder matrix.

expected_human_approval_gates.synthetic_rationale: Two-person integrity for production-facing dependency moves even under expedited SLA.

expected_final_report.title: Supply-chain remediation briefing — from CVE rumor to validated patch with audit trail
expected_final_report.synthetic_executive_summary: We merged authoritative vulnerability cues with realistic application context, prioritized with transparent math understandable to boards, routed work through sanctioned change tooling, and closed the loop with third-party styled validation—all narrated without operational secrets or exploit scaffolding.

golden_path_demo.executive_timeline_summary: From intel refresh to CAB-trackable ticket in tens of minutes, then through controlled execution and validation snapshots suitable for auditors—demonstrating SecureWatch as orchestration membrane across AppSec + IT Ops + SOC.

expected_database_outputs.business_outcome: Demonstrates linkage between advisory identifiers and tenant asset graph.
Single prioritized row with SLA clock and CAB references.
Shows closed-loop remediation with validation artifacts.
Dual-control approvals recorded for expedited tier.
End-to-end history for SOC 2 / ISO auditors.

expected_dashboard_summary.headline: Critical dependency debt collapsed into governed execution investors trust
kpis: Median time from CVE correlation → approved change record (golden cohort)
Percent of criticals with ITSM linkage vs spreadsheets
Patch validation completeness score (automated scanners + manual sign-off stubs)
Incident of repeat findings on same semantic component (should trend downward)
Engineering interruption minutes vs traditional war-room anecdote benchmarks
expected_dashboard_summary.narrative: Dashboard overlays vulnerability velocity with organizational friction metrics so Series B/C narratives sound like disciplined operations—not heroics.
